summary: http-uploader-dev executes unsolicited commands on installer machines at npm install
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "bun run index.js"`, and index.js unconditionally spawns a GUI calculator on the installer's machine based on `os.platform()`: `execSync('open -a Calculator')` on macOS, `execSync('xcalc || gnome-calculator || kcalc')` on Linux, and `execSync('calc.exe')` on Windows (index.js lines 9-17). The behavior has no relationship to the package's stated 'http uploader' purpose and demonstrates arbitrary command execution on any machine that runs `npm install http-uploader-dev`. The mechanism is identical to a malicious payload — only the chosen command is benign. Package metadata reinforces the throwaway/PoC profile: author `sleep`, generic description `uploader dev`, and homepage `https://git.hfaf.com/urlaa` on a non-standard host. The preinstall additionally requires Bun (`bun run`) to be present on the installer's PATH, an alternate-runtime invocation pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
